title: Instantiate host2 from workshop image
description:
Create the second workshop host from the snapshot, attach reserved IP #2.

  gcloud compute instances create host2 \
      --image=devday-workshop-v1 --zone=us-central1-a \
      --machine-type=n2-standard-32 \
      --address=<reserved-ip-2>

Verify after boot: hostname is 'host2', /etc/lab-passphrase exists with the right value, /usr/local/bin/create-account is present, admin@host2 can ssh to admin@host1 without a password (and vice versa, since both inherit the same SSH key from the image).
close_reason: Not needed under single-host (n2-standard-64) architecture. No host2 to instantiate; disaster recovery is via snapshot (devday-bgd) attached to the cold-spare IP, not multi-host stamp-out.

title: Snapshot host1 → workshop image (disaster recovery)
description:
Once host1 is fully hand-configured and validated (create-account smoke test passes), capture its disk as a GCP image. This is the DR path, not a multi-host deploy mechanism: if host1 dies mid-workshop, spin up a replacement from this image and attach the spare reserved IP (devday-host2, 35.232.23.141) — attendees switch from devday1 to devday2 and keep going.

  gcloud compute images create devday-workshop-v1 \
      --source-disk=host1 --source-disk-zone=us-central1-a

Increment the version suffix on subsequent re-snapshots so you can roll back if a re-bake breaks something. Re-snapshot whenever you materially change the host config (new ltvm release, new passphrase, etc).

title: Hand-configure the workshop host
description:
Spin up an n2-standard-64 Rocky 9 VM in us-central1-a (512 GB pd-balanced disk recommended to fit ltvm build artifacts + ccache + pre-cloned Lustre tree), attach reserved IP #1 (34.61.47.200, devday-host1), and hand-configure it. Steps:

1. Set hostname to 'host1' (matters for any tooling that keys on hostname -s)
2. Create the admin user with passphrase blew-hurry-throughout-rate, NOPASSWD sudo, /home/admin
3. Write /etc/lab-passphrase (root, mode 600) containing: blew-hurry-throughout-rate
4. Install ltvm dependencies, clone lustre-test-vms to /opt or ~admin, run sudo ./ltvm install
5. sudo ltvm fetch rocky9
6. Clone lug-devday-2026 to ~admin/lug-devday-2026
7. Install bin/create-account → /usr/local/bin/create-account (mode 755)
8. Pre-clone Lustre to /etc/skel/lustre-release so each new attendee account gets a fresh copy in their home, OR document that attendees clone themselves (decide based on time/bandwidth tradeoffs)
9. Sanity check: as admin, run create-account on a throwaway test user (close out with userdel after)

Once done, devday-bgd is the disaster-recovery snapshot step — take it after validation so a mid-workshop host death isn't a disaster.

title: Set up DNS for devday1 and devday2.mulberrytree.us
description:
Add two A records under mulberrytree.us:

  devday1.mulberrytree.us A  34.61.47.200   # devday-host1 (live)
  devday2.mulberrytree.us A  35.232.23.141  # devday-host2 (cold spare)

Both records go in *before* the workshop so disaster-recovery failover is just 'spin up a new VM with the spare IP attached' with zero DNS propagation wait.

title: Reserve 4 static public IPs in GCP (us-central1)
description: Reserve 4 static external IPs in the ltvm-workshop-playground GCP project, region us-central1. Two will back the live workshop hosts (host1, host2); two are cold spares for failover (host3, host4) so a dead VM is just an IP-attach away from being back online — no DNS propagation wait. See WORKSHOP.md for the rationale.
notes:
Reserved 2026-04-11 in us-central1, Premium tier:

  devday-host1  34.61.47.200
  devday-host2  35.232.23.141
  devday-host3  35.224.134.233
  devday-host4  34.171.126.149

These are the IPs to feed into the DNS records (devday-9ou) and to attach to host1/host2 when instantiating from the workshop image (devday-bgd / devday-saq). host3 and host4 are cold spares for failover.

title: Generate workshop passphrase
description: Generate a strong diceware passphrase (4+ words) to use as the shared workshop login. This value gets baked into /etc/lab-passphrase on the host image and is what attendees see on the slide. Store the value durably (1Password / paper) — if you lose it, you have to rebuild the image.

title: Stage agent config (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md) for attendee homes
description:
Workshop attendees who use Claude Code (or other AI coding agents) should land in a /home/<user> with a baseline AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md that gives the agent the right context: ltvm is the build/deploy tool, /home/<user>/lustre-release is the source tree, gerrit is the push target, etc.

Approach: drop a CLAUDE.md and an AGENTS.md into /etc/skel on the golden image so that every new useradd -m gets a fresh copy. Content should explain:

- This is a Lustre dev workshop on a shared GCP host (so don't blow up the shared resources)
- ltvm is the test-VM tool: build-lustre, create, deploy, exec
- ~/lustre-release is the Lustre source tree
- git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/master is the patch submission path
- VMs are visible across attendees via 'ltvm list' so the agent should be careful with destroy
- 'sudo ltvm <whatever>' is normal — passwordless sudo is on for attendees

Cribs from lustre-test-vms-v2/SUGGESTED-AGENTS.md (the template that ships in the ltvm repo for users to append to their workspace agent config) — that file is already designed for exactly this case.

title: Get limited Gerrit/JIRA accounts ready for attendees
description:
Pre-workshop work: figure out the path for attendees to get accounts on review.whamcloud.com (Gerrit) and the Whamcloud JIRA without requiring full Whamcloud-employee provisioning. Options to investigate:

- Self-serve Gerrit signup via GitHub/Google SSO (already exists per ATTENDEE_FLOW.md, just needs verification that it actually works for non-Whamcloud users)
- Whether Whamcloud JIRA needs anything beyond Gerrit signin
- Whether there's a 'limited' or 'sandbox' role we should be steering attendees toward to avoid them filing real bugs accidentally
- Whether attendees actually NEED JIRA at all for the workshop scope, or if Gerrit is sufficient

Output: a confirmed pre-workshop instruction block for ATTENDEE_FLOW.md (or a confirmation that the existing block is correct).  Loop in jhammond / sihara / whoever owns Whamcloud dev infra.

title: Smoke-test create-account end-to-end
description:
Once the workshop host is hand-configured, validate the full attendee bootstrap path end-to-end on the real environment:

1. ssh admin@devday1.mulberrytree.us with the workshop passphrase
2. Run: create-account testuser 'Test User' test@example.com
3. Paste a real SSH pubkey when prompted
4. Verify provisioning completes without cross-host errors (single-host model)
5. Log out, ssh testuser@devday1.mulberrytree.us — should succeed via key auth, no password prompt
6. Verify gitconfig: testuser@host1$ git config --global --get user.name (should print 'Test User')
7. Verify sudo: testuser@host1$ sudo -n true (should succeed without prompt)
8. Run create-account again with same username — should refuse with the 'already exists' error
9. Run create-account --force-password testuser ... — should succeed and reset
10. Verify that a DIFFERENT pubkey supplied on --force-password REPLACES the old one (not appends)
11. Cleanup: as admin, sudo userdel -r testuser; sudo rm /etc/sudoers.d/testuser

If anything in this checklist fails, file a bug bead and fix before opening the workshop.
